title: Visual Studio May Update – Plan, Review, Refine
description:
There’s a particular rhythm to good development work: you think, you try, you check, you adjust. This month’s Visual Studio update leans into that rhythm. Whether you’re sketching an approach with the Plan agent before touching a single file, reviewing a wave of changes across many files, or fine-tuning the context Copilot has to work […]

title: Plan Before You Build: Introducing the Plan agent in Visual Studio
description:
You ask Copilot to tackle something big, it gets to work, and a dozen file changes later you realize you had a completely different approach in mind. The code isn’t wrong… it just isn’t what you were going for. Last year, we introduced planning as a feature in Agent mode to help with exactly this. […]
The post Plan Before You Build: Introducing the Plan agent in Visual Studio appeared first on Visual Studio Blog.
creator: Rachel Kang (SHE/HER)

title: Introducing a Security Support Policy for the Kotlin Standard Library
description: Upgrade rhythms vary significantly among Kotlin’s user base. Some teams update whenever a new release lands without a second thought. On the other hand, a team inside a regulated organization moves on a multi-quarter cycle and treats every dependency as something that has to be reviewed, approved, and then frozen in production for a while. […]
creator: Anton Yalyshev

title: How Four Teams Stopped Postponing the Refactoring They Knew They Needed
description: As an engineering leader, you don’t need to be told your codebase needs attention. The issue isn’t awareness – it’s the rational risk calculation that follows. For four teams, that calculation kept producing the same answer: defer. They found a way out not by avoiding the calculation, but by changing what went into it. To […]
creator: Colette Des Georges

title: What’s New for C++ Developers in Visual Studio 2026 (18.1 – 18.6)
description:
Visual Studio 2026 versions 18.1 through 18.6 deliver a wave of improvements for C++ developers. For MSVC Build Tools, the v14.51 release is now generally available with runtime performance gains and improved C++23 conformance. We also introduced Sample Profile Guided Optimization to help you improve the runtime performance of your C/C++ programs. In the IDE, […]
The post What’s New for C++ Developers in Visual Studio 2026 (18.1 – 18.6) appeared first on C++ Team Blog.
creator: Augustin Popa

title: 새로운 세상인가, 새로운 거품인가?
description: CB Insights의 State of Venture는 아마도 전 세계 VC와 이 분야에 종사하는 많은 분이 가장 필수적으로 읽는 보고서일 것이다. 나도 분기마다 이 보고서를 읽고 지난 분기에 글로벌 벤처 시장에서 어떤 일이 있었는지 정리하고, 우리가 투자하는 한국 시장 관련 내용이 있으면 찾아서 읽어본다. 한 분기를 정리하는 보고서라서 내용이 다양하지만, 30분 정도만 투자하면 글로벌 벤처 시장에서 돈이(...)

title: How AI Agents Can Work with TeamCity
description: TL;DR: At some point, we crossed an interesting threshold. title: SilverTorch: Index as Model — A New Retrieval Paradigm for Recommendation Systems
description:
We’re introducing SilverTorch, a reimagining of recommendation systems that unifies all retrieval components for user generated content under a unified architecture.  SilverTorch shows up to 23.7x higher throughput compared to the state-of-the-art approaches. It’s also showing 20.9x more compute cost efficiency compared to a CPU-based solution while also improving accuracy.  Our research paper, “SilverTorch: A [...]
Read More...
The post SilverTorch: Index as Model — A New Retrieval Paradigm for Recommendation Systems appeared first on Engineering at Meta.

title: KotlinConf’26 Keynote Highlights: Advances in Language Design, Tooling, AI-Driven Workflows, and Multiplatform Development
description: Kotlin turns 15 this year, and it really is everywhere. title: What Happens When You Give AI Agents the Map of Your Code’s Coverage?
